text.title: Chunking Basics — Procedure
text.summary: 5-step procedure: detect content type → pick splitter + band → measure with tiktoken → tag metadata → benchmark on ≥5 queries.

section.title: Step 1 — Detect content type
section.p: Inspect file extension and the first 200 bytes. Code → chunking-code-ast. Markdown / HTML → chunking-document-structure. Prose → continue with recursive/sentence splitter from this methodology.

section.title: Step 2 — Pick splitter + size band
section.p: Apply rule r2-default-band. General prose: 500/50. Legal/technical: 1200/150. QA corpora: 300/60. Hold band constant across pipeline version; record in metadata.strategy + metadata.version.

section.title: Step 3 — Measure with tiktoken
section.p: For every chunk, run `tiktoken.get_encoding(enc).encode(text)` and store `token_count = len(tokens)`. Reject any chunk whose token_count exceeds the embedding model's input cap.

section.title: Step 4 — Tag metadata at creation
section.p: Attach `{id=md5(source+index+version), source, page, chunk_index, strategy, version, token_count, overlap}` on the chunk object. Do not defer to a later pass.

section.title: Step 5 — Benchmark on ≥5 queries
section.p: Run the planned retrieval queries against the new index. Compare recall@k and MRR with the prior strategy if any. If recall drops more than 5%, do not roll out; tune band and repeat.
